message:
fix(fp-frame): to_dict orient='index' rejects duplicate index (frankenpandas-sewx)

pandas raises ValueError when DataFrame.to_dict(orient='index') is
called on a frame whose index has duplicate labels — the result
dict keys would collide and silently drop rows. The previous
implementation used BTreeMap::insert which overwrote on collision,
losing data without any signal to the caller.

Adds an explicit has_duplicates() guard returning
FrameError::CompatibilityRejected with a descriptive message,
matching the policy already applied to to_json(orient='index') at
fp-frame/src/lib.rs:5155.

Two tests: one exercises the new rejection path on a duplicate
[1, 2, 1] index, one confirms unique-index round-trips still work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
